mission.title: kubeflow: Ray on Kubeflow - Asking for user and contributor interest / comments
mission.description: Ray on Kubeflow - Asking for user and contributor interest / comments. Requested by 14+ users.

steps.title: Check current kubeflow deployment
steps.description:
Verify your kubeflow version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n kubeflow -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kubeflow
helm list -n kubeflow 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working kubeflow installation.

steps.title: Review kubeflow configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant kubeflow configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n kubeflow -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kubeflow
kubectl get configmap -n kubeflow -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=kubeflow
```
**Why you need this feature:**
Ray provides a preprocessing, training, tuning and serving framework and a growing number of data scientist are using it i.e. the Ray Community has nearly 5,000 slack members.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Ray on Kubeflow - Asking for user and contributor interest
steps.description:
I have already started a few days ago with the architecture.
There are some limitations that need to be mitigated so satisfy the new /contrib requirements merged a few days ago.

1. Enforce Runasnonroot for all images: Remove the root rights from the rayworker according to https://github.com/ray-project/ray/issues/14077#issuecomment-1283208595. I think udocker might be most promising. Most other images should already be compatible.   can you take care of that?
2. Namespace isolation: We need

See the fix PR for details: linked PR

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n kubeflow -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kubeflow
kubectl get events -n kubeflow --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Ray on Kubeflow - Asking for user and contributor interest" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
I have already started a few days ago with the architecture.
There are some limitations that need to be mitigated so satisfy the new /contrib requirements merged a few days ago.

1. Enforce Runasnonroot for all images: Remove the root rights from the rayworker according to https://github.com/ray-project/ray/issues/14077#issuecomment-1283208595. I think udocker might be most promising.

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with kubeflow installed or the issue environment reproducible.
